title: CompositeChannelFactory routes IChannel instantiation by config.type, letting Swarm and GQL factories coexist behind one SyncManager interface.
content:
## Setup sequence

`createSwarmSyncBuilder(logger, jwtHandler)` returns `{ syncBuilder, compositeFactory, registerGqlFactory }`:

1. `CompositeChannelFactory` created with `"swarm"` registered.
2. `SyncBuilder.withChannelFactory(compositeFactory)`.

Then wired into the reactor:

```
ReactorBuilder
  .withSync(syncBuilder)       // proper builder API
  .withJwtHandler(jwtHandler)
```

`buildModule()` runs:
- Creates queue with the document-model resolver.
- `SyncManager.startup()` recreates persisted remotes.
- Swarm remotes work immediately because the factory is already registered.

After `buildModule()`: `registerGqlFactory(reactorModule.queue)` registers `"gql"` on the same `CompositeChannelFactory`, so GQL remotes can now be added via `addRemoteDrive()`.

## Dispatch

```
compositeFactory.instance(config)
  config.type === "gql"   -> GqlRequestChannelFactory -> GqlRequestChannel
  config.type === "swarm" -> SwarmChannelFactory    -> SwarmChannel
```

SyncManager remains channel-type-agnostic; it only touches `outbox`, `inbox`, and `deadLetter` on whatever channel object it receives.
description: createSwarmSyncBuilder wires a CompositeChannelFactory into ReactorBuilder.withSync(); after buildModule(), registerGqlFactory adds GQL support without disturbing Swarm.
